skill.name: image-to-text-pdf
skill.description: Convert a finished raster image, especially a generated poster or visual resume, into an image-based PDF with an additional selectable, copyable, searchable text layer. Use when the image is the final visual layout, when recreating that layout in PPT, HTML, or LaTeX would be fragile, and when the user needs both a final invisible-text PDF and a visible inspection PDF for checking OCR or text-layer placement.
skill.summary: Add selectable and searchable text layers to finished raster-image PDFs.
skill.author: zjsxply

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium findings, but review found no malicious intent, network use, credential access, or prompt injection. The Ruby shell execution and weak cryptography reports are false positives caused by markdown code fences, inline code, argparse text, regex ranges, and Unicode ranges. The remaining real risk is low: the skill runs local Python scripts that read user-selected files and write output PDFs or layout JSON.

low_findings.title: Local File Read and Write Capability
low_findings.description: The helper scripts read user-provided image, layout, OCR, and source-text files, then create output PDFs or layout JSON. This is expected for the skill, but users should choose paths carefully because parent directories are created and outputs may overwrite existing files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are directly visible and are required for the skill's document conversion workflow. I did not find evidence of network transmission, hidden persistence, or unauthorized path selection.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby shell execution findings are markdown code fences, inline field names, or example commands for running local scripts. They do not execute automatically and do not contain shell interpolation or remote commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation blocks and inline code syntax, not Ruby backtick operators in executable source. The only commands shown are explicit local Python invocations for this skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high severity weak cryptography reports do not point to cryptographic algorithms. The cited lines are skill description text, argparse setup, text extraction helpers, and regex or Unicode handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or related cryptographic call appears at the cited locations. The static pattern matched ordinary words and parser code rather than security-sensitive cryptography.

low_findings.title: Unicode Escape Findings Are CJK Range Checks
low_findings.description: The Unicode escape findings occur in a helper that detects CJK characters for OCR word joining. This is not obfuscation and does not hide executable content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Unicode escapes are literal character range boundaries used by a clearly named CJK detection function. There is no encoded payload, dynamic evaluation, or hidden control flow.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Error Context
low_findings.description: The low severity reconnaissance report points to an exception message that includes rectangle coordinates when text does not fit. It does not collect system details, environment values, host data, or user secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code formats local layout geometry into an error string for debugging PDF text placement. I found no evidence of system enumeration or data exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Finished images are hard to search, select, or reuse as PDF content. This skill adds an OCR-guided text layer while preserving the original raster design.
actual_capabilities: Creates an image-based PDF that preserves the source raster image as the visible layer.
Adds invisible text boxes so PDF text can be selected, copied, and searched.
Generates a visible debug PDF for checking text placement before delivery.
Converts common word-level OCR JSON into line-level layout items.
Supports pixel and relative coordinates, bounding boxes, polygons, and optional Unicode fonts.
limitations: It does not perform OCR by itself; it expects OCR or vision output from another tool.
Rotated, curved, or very small decorative text may require manual layout correction.
The hidden text layer may not visually match stylized text exactly.
Users must provide valid local paths and dependencies such as PyMuPDF and Pillow.

use_cases.title: Deliver Searchable Visual Resumes
use_cases.description: Convert a final resume image into a PDF that preserves the design and supports text selection.

use_cases.title: Archive Generated Posters
use_cases.description: Create searchable poster PDFs from generated artwork while keeping the image layout unchanged.

use_cases.title: Inspect OCR Text Layers
use_cases.description: Generate a visible review PDF that highlights each embedded text span for quality checks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has an image and layout JSON ready.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-to-text-pdf to create a searchable PDF from this image and layout file. Also create a debug PDF for review.

prompt_templates.title: OCR Layout Preparation
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has word-level OCR output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this OCR word JSON into a line-level layout for image-to-text-pdf. Use the image dimensions I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Correct OCR with Source Text
prompt_templates.scenario: A generated image has known source text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a corrected text-layer layout from this OCR output. Use my source text as authoritative, but keep the OCR box positions.

prompt_templates.title: Debug and Refine Placement
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants production-quality text alignment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the final PDF and inspection PDF, then identify any shifted boxes or copy errors that need layout changes.

output_examples.input: A generated visual resume image plus corrected line-level layout data.
output_examples.output: A final PDF that looks like the image and allows resume text to be copied and searched.

output_examples.input: Word-level OCR output from a poster image.
output_examples.output: A cleaned layout file with grouped text lines and bounding boxes ready for PDF composition.

output_examples.input: A multilingual image and a Unicode font file.
output_examples.output: A searchable PDF with embedded text using the provided font for better extraction support.

best_practices: Use the final raster image as the visual source of truth.
Keep layout items at line level unless paragraph selection is required.
Always inspect the debug PDF before sharing the final file.
anti_patterns: Do not rebuild the design in slides, HTML, or LaTeX after image generation.
Do not trust OCR text when authoritative source text is available.
Do not embed tiny decorative text unless the user needs it searchable.

faq.question: Does this skill run OCR?
faq.answer: No. It uses OCR or vision results from another tool to build and place the PDF text layer.

faq.question: Will the PDF look different from the source image?
faq.answer: The final PDF should preserve the image as the visible layer. Only the hidden text layer is added.

faq.question: Why does it create a debug PDF?
faq.answer: The debug PDF shows visible text boxes so users can verify alignment and copy text before delivery.

faq.question: Can it handle non-Latin text?
faq.answer: Yes, but a suitable Unicode font should be provided for reliable text embedding and extraction.

faq.question: What input files are needed?
faq.answer: The main workflow needs an image and layout data. OCR conversion also needs word-level OCR JSON.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found no malicious behavior, but users should understand that local scripts read and write chosen files.
